title: Using open telemetry in Golang
subtitle: In such a way that traces and metrics are emitted to the console.
excerpt: In such a way that traces and metrics are emitted to the console.

content.html: In such a way that traces and metrics are emitted to the console.
You can use in other framework too look like gin

content.text: Get dependency of open telemetry in Golang

content.html: Initialize OpenTelemetry SDK for Go. Replace Endpoint and URLPath with the endpoint obtained in the Prerequisites section.
You can submit data by using OpenTelemetry SDK for Go or forward data by using the open source OpenTelemetry Collector.

items: If you submit data by using OpenTelemetry SDK for Go, replace Endpoint and URLPath with the endpoint obtained in the Prerequisites section.
If you forward data by using the open source OpenTelemetry Collector, replace Endpoint with the IP address of your on-premises service and delete the headers

content.code: exporter, err := stdouttrace.New(stdouttrace.WithPrettyPrint())  if err != nil {  log.Fatal(err)}

content.text: if you had a “ failed to build resolver: passthrough: received empty target in Build() ” error in tests, then put below code for your open telemetry in test cases:

content.html: When using OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT, exporters MUST construct per-signal URLs as described below.
Timeout: Maximum time the OTLP exporter will wait for each batch export.
Protocol: The transport protocol. Options MUST be one of: grpc, http/protobuf, http/json. See Specify Protocol for more details.
